-- ===== importer logic =====
-- 7th Sea Deck Importer -- tile logic.
--
-- The generated card database (CARD_DB, ALIASES, UTF8_FOLD, IMAGE_BASE,
-- BACK_URL, SCHEMA_VERSION) is prepended to this file by cmd/ttsgen. This
-- half is hand-written and mirrors the Go reference parser in
-- internal/tts (parse.go, normalize.go) line for line -- Tabletop Simulator
-- cannot run our tests, so the Go suite loads this file in a real Lua VM and
-- checks normalize()/parseList() against the Go implementations.
--
-- Pure logic (normalize, parseList, buildDeckObject) touches no TTS API, so
-- it is testable off-table. Only the spawn/UI half calls into TTS.

-- ===== string helpers =====

function trim(s)
  return (string.gsub(s, "^%s*(.-)%s*$", "%1"))
end

-- normalize mirrors internal/tts Normalize: fold accented UTF-8 to ASCII,
-- lowercase, reduce every non-alphanumeric run to a single space, trim.
function normalize(s)
  if s == nil then return "" end
  s = string.lower(s)
  for seq, base in pairs(UTF8_FOLD) do
    s = string.gsub(s, seq, base)
  end
  local out, prevSpace = {}, true
  for i = 1, #s do
    local c = string.byte(s, i)
    if (c >= 97 and c <= 122) or (c >= 48 and c <= 57) then
      out[#out + 1] = string.char(c)
      prevSpace = false
    elseif not prevSpace then
      out[#out + 1] = " "
      prevSpace = true
    end
  end
  return (string.gsub(table.concat(out), "%s+$", ""))
end

local function isPrintingID(tok)
  return tok ~= nil and string.match(tok, "^%w+%-%d+$") ~= nil
end

-- resolveRef mirrors internal/tts resolveRef: a printing id in the text wins;
-- otherwise resolve the name, preferring a Leader printing when wantLeader.
-- Returns id, or nil plus a reason.
function resolveRef(ref, wantLeader)
  ref = trim(ref)
  if ref == "" then return nil, "empty card reference" end

  local head = string.match(ref, "^(%S+)")
  if isPrintingID(head) then
    if CARD_DB[head] then return head end
    return nil, "unknown printing id " .. head
  end

  local ids = ALIASES[normalize(ref)]
  if ids == nil then return nil, "unknown card name" end
  for _, id in ipairs(ids) do
    if (CARD_DB[id].deck == "Leader") == wantLeader then return id end
  end
  return ids[1]
end

-- parseList mirrors internal/tts ParseList. Returns a table:
--   { title=, leader=<pid or nil>, cards={ {pid=,count=}... }, problems={ {line=,reason=} } }
function parseList(text)
  local list = { title = "", leader = nil, cards = {}, problems = {} }
  if text == nil then return list end

  -- Strip a UTF-8 BOM and normalize newlines.
  if string.sub(text, 1, 3) == "\239\187\191" then text = string.sub(text, 4) end
  text = string.gsub(text, "
", "
")
  text = string.gsub(text, "", "
")

  local index = {} -- pid -> position in list.cards
  local function add(pid, count)
    local at = index[pid]
    if at then
      list.cards[at].count = list.cards[at].count + count
    else
      list.cards[#list.cards + 1] = { pid = pid, count = count }
      index[pid] = #list.cards
    end
  end

  local leaderName, cardsStarted = nil, false

  for raw in string.gmatch(text .. "
", "([^
]*)
") do
    local line = trim(raw)
    if line == "" then
      -- skip
    elseif string.sub(line, 1, 1) == "#" or string.sub(line, 1, 2) == "//" then
      local comment = trim(string.gsub(line, "^[#/]+", ""))
      if list.title == "" and comment ~= "" then list.title = comment end
    else
      local count, ref = string.match(line, "^(%d+)%s*[xX]?%s+(.+)$")
      local isHeader = string.match(line, "^[%a][%w ]*:") ~= nil
      if count == nil and isHeader then
        local key, val = string.match(line, "^([^:]+):%s*(.*)$")
        key = string.lower(trim(key))
        val = trim(val)
        if key == "deckname" or key == "deck" or key == "title" or key == "name" then
          if list.title == "" then list.title = val end
        elseif key == "leader" then
          if val ~= "" then leaderName = val end
        end
      elseif count == nil then
        -- A bare name before any counted line is the tournament leader;
        -- after cards begin, trailing prose ends the list.
        if (not cardsStarted) and leaderName == nil then
          leaderName = line
        else
          break
        end
      else
        cardsStarted = true
        local n = tonumber(count)
        if n == nil or n <= 0 then
          list.problems[#list.problems + 1] = { line = line, reason = "bad quantity" }
        else
          local id, reason = resolveRef(ref, false)
          if id == nil then
            list.problems[#list.problems + 1] = { line = line, reason = reason }
          else
            add(id, n)
          end
        end
      end
    end
  end

  -- Resolve the leader: an explicit leader line wins, else promote whichever
  -- imported card the database calls a Leader printing.
  if leaderName ~= nil then
    local id, reason = resolveRef(leaderName, true)
    if id == nil then
      list.problems[#list.problems + 1] = { line = leaderName, reason = reason }
    else
      list.leader = id
    end
  end
  if list.leader == nil then
    for i, e in ipairs(list.cards) do
      if CARD_DB[e.pid] and CARD_DB[e.pid].deck == "Leader" then
        list.leader = e.pid
        table.remove(list.cards, i)
        break
      end
    end
  end
  return list
end

function totalCards(list)
  local n = 0
  for _, e in ipairs(list.cards) do n = n + e.count end
  return n
end

-- ===== deck object construction =====
--
-- Each distinct printing gets its own 1x1 CustomDeck sheet. TTS card ids are
-- sheetIndex*100 + cardOnSheet; with one card per sheet the on-sheet index is
-- always 0, so CardID = sheetIndex*100.

local function cardObject(pid, cardID, sheetKey)
  local c = CARD_DB[pid]
  local desc = c.text or ""
  return {
    Name = "CardCustom",
    Nickname = c.name,
    Description = desc,
    CardID = cardID,
    Transform = {
      posX = 0, posY = 0, posZ = 0,
      rotX = 0, rotY = 180, rotZ = 180, -- face down
      scaleX = 1, scaleY = 1, scaleZ = 1,
    },
    CustomDeck = {
      [tostring(sheetKey)] = {
        FaceURL = IMAGE_BASE .. pid .. ".jpg",
        BackURL = BACK_URL,
        NumWidth = 1, NumHeight = 1,
        BackIsHidden = true, UniqueBack = false, Type = 0,
      },
    },
  }
end

-- buildDeckObject turns parsed entries into a TTS Deck saved-object table (not
-- JSON). Returns nil when there are fewer than two cards -- TTS needs a Deck
-- to hold at least two, so a single card is spawned on its own by the caller.
function buildDeckObject(entries, nickname)
  local totalCopies = 0
  for _, e in ipairs(entries) do totalCopies = totalCopies + e.count end
  if totalCopies < 2 then return nil end

  local sheetOf = {}
  local nextSheet = 1
  local customDeck = {}
  local deckIDs = {}
  local contained = {}

  for _, e in ipairs(entries) do
    local sheet = sheetOf[e.pid]
    if sheet == nil then
      sheet = nextSheet
      nextSheet = nextSheet + 1
      sheetOf[e.pid] = sheet
      local co = cardObject(e.pid, sheet * 100, sheet)
      customDeck[tostring(sheet)] = co.CustomDeck[tostring(sheet)]
    end
    for _ = 1, e.count do
      deckIDs[#deckIDs + 1] = sheet * 100
      contained[#contained + 1] = cardObject(e.pid, sheet * 100, sheet)
    end
  end

  return {
    Name = "Deck",
    Nickname = nickname or "",
    Transform = {
      posX = 0, posY = 0, posZ = 0,
      rotX = 0, rotY = 180, rotZ = 180,
      scaleX = 1, scaleY = 1, scaleZ = 1,
    },
    DeckIDs = deckIDs,
    CustomDeck = customDeck,
    ContainedObjects = contained,
  }
end

-- singleCardObject builds a standalone face-up card (used for the leader).
function singleCardObject(pid, faceUp)
  local co = cardObject(pid, 100, 1)
  co.Transform.rotZ = faceUp and 0 or 180
  return co
end

-- ===== table code + relay polling (pure parts) =====

-- Unambiguous alphabet: no I/L/O/0/1, so a code read off the tile is easy to
-- type into the app without confusion.
CODE_ALPHABET = "ABCDEFGHJKMNPQRSTUVWXYZ23456789"

function newTableCode()
  local t = {}
  for i = 1, 6 do
    local n = math.random(1, #CODE_ALPHABET)
    t[i] = string.sub(CODE_ALPHABET, n, n)
  end
  return table.concat(t)
end

-- playersToFetch returns the players whose manifest timestamp is newer than
-- the recorded watermark -- the decks uploaded since we last spawned them.
-- Kept pure (no TTS API) so the "spawn each delivery exactly once" rule is
-- unit-tested off-table.
function playersToFetch(manifest, watermarks)
  local out = {}
  for player, ts in pairs(manifest) do
    if ts > (watermarks[player] or 0) then out[#out + 1] = player end
  end
  return out
end

-- ===== TTS integration (not exercised off-table) =====

pastedText = ""
tableCode = nil
relayURL = nil
listening = false
watermarks = {}   -- player -> last spawned manifest timestamp
pollHandle = nil

-- Where spawned objects land, relative to the tile. Tuned in-game.
local DECK_OFFSET = { x = 2.2, y = 1.5, z = 0 }
local LEADER_OFFSET = { x = -0.6, y = 1.5, z = 0 }
local POLL_SECONDS = 5

function onLoad(saved)
  if saved ~= nil and saved ~= "" then
    local ok, state = pcall(function() return JSON.decode(saved) end)
    if ok and type(state) == "table" then
      tableCode = state.code
      relayURL = state.relay
      watermarks = state.watermarks or {}
    end
  end
  -- A fresh tile (no persisted code) mints its own, so every table is a
  -- separate mailbox and no code is ever shared by baking it into the save.
  if tableCode == nil or tableCode == "" then
    math.randomseed(os.time())
    tableCode = newTableCode()
  end
  if relayURL == nil then relayURL = RELAY_URL end
  createUI()
end

function onSave()
  return JSON.encode({ code = tableCode, relay = relayURL, watermarks = watermarks })
end

-- createUI lays the controls out in a single centered vertical column. TTS
-- multiplies element POSITIONS by the object's scale, so every coordinate
-- here is small and stays within the tile face; keeping x = 0 for all of them
-- rules out the horizontal overlap that side-by-side buttons caused. The tile
-- is a tall portrait (see EmitTile's scale) to give the column room. The relay
-- URL is baked into RELAY_URL, so it is intentionally NOT shown here.
function createUI()
  self.clearInputs()
  self.clearButtons()

  self.createInput({
    label = "7th Sea Deck List (paste, or Fetch by table code)",
    input_function = "onTextInput",
    function_owner = self,
    alignment = 2,
    position = { 0, 0.2, -0.62 },
    width = 1050, height = 360,
    font_size = 44,
    tab = 2,
    value = pastedText,
  })

  self.createButton({
    label = "Import pasted",
    click_function = "onImportClick",
    function_owner = self,
    position = { 0, 0.2, -0.10 },
    width = 620, height = 150,
    font_size = 78,
    color = { 0.2, 0.55, 0.25 },
    font_color = { 1, 1, 1 },
  })

  self.createButton({
    label = "Table code:  " .. (tableCode or "----"),
    click_function = "onCopyCode",
    function_owner = self,
    position = { 0, 0.2, 0.18 },
    width = 1050, height = 150,
    font_size = 78,
    color = { 0.12, 0.12, 0.12 },
    font_color = { 1, 0.85, 0.4 },
    tooltip = "Type this code into the app to send decks to this table.",
  })

  self.createButton({
    label = listening and "Listening: ON  (tap to stop)" or "Listen: off  (tap to start)",
    click_function = "onToggleListen",
    function_owner = self,
    position = { 0, 0.2, 0.46 },
    width = 1050, height = 150,
    font_size = 66,
    color = listening and { 0.2, 0.5, 0.2 } or { 0.4, 0.22, 0.22 },
    font_color = { 1, 1, 1 },
  })

  self.createButton({
    label = "Fetch now",
    click_function = "onFetchNow",
    function_owner = self,
    position = { 0, 0.2, 0.74 },
    width = 620, height = 150,
    font_size = 78,
    color = { 0.2, 0.35, 0.55 },
    font_color = { 1, 1, 1 },
  })
end

function onTextInput(_, _, text)
  pastedText = text
end

function onImportClick()
  importText(pastedText)
end

function onCopyCode()
  broadcastToAll("This table's code is " .. tostring(tableCode)
    .. " -- enter it in the app to send decks here.", { 1, 0.9, 0.5 })
end

function onToggleListen()
  listening = not listening
  if listening then
    startPolling()
  else
    stopPolling()
  end
  createUI()
end

function onFetchNow()
  pollOnce()
end

-- importText parses and spawns. Kept separate from the click handler so the
-- fetch path (M3) can feed relay text through the same pipeline.
function importText(text)
  local list = parseList(text)
  local total = totalCards(list)

  if total == 0 and list.leader == nil then
    broadcastToAll("Deck Importer: nothing to import (no recognized cards).", { 1, 0.6, 0.2 })
    reportProblems(list)
    return
  end

  local origin = self.getPosition()
  local nick = list.title
  if nick == "" then nick = "Imported Deck" end

  local deck = buildDeckObject(list.cards, nick)
  if deck ~= nil then
    deck.Transform.posX = origin.x + DECK_OFFSET.x
    deck.Transform.posY = origin.y + DECK_OFFSET.y
    deck.Transform.posZ = origin.z + DECK_OFFSET.z
    spawnObjectJSON({ json = JSON.encode(deck) })
  elseif #list.cards == 1 then
    local co = singleCardObject(list.cards[1].pid, false)
    co.Transform.posX = origin.x + DECK_OFFSET.x
    co.Transform.posY = origin.y + DECK_OFFSET.y
    co.Transform.posZ = origin.z + DECK_OFFSET.z
    spawnObjectJSON({ json = JSON.encode(co) })
  end

  if list.leader ~= nil then
    local lead = singleCardObject(list.leader, true)
    lead.Transform.posX = origin.x + LEADER_OFFSET.x
    lead.Transform.posY = origin.y + LEADER_OFFSET.y
    lead.Transform.posZ = origin.z + LEADER_OFFSET.z
    spawnObjectJSON({ json = JSON.encode(lead) })
  end

  local msg = "Imported " .. total .. " card" .. (total == 1 and "" or "s")
  if list.leader ~= nil then
    msg = msg .. " + leader " .. CARD_DB[list.leader].name
  end
  if nick ~= "Imported Deck" then msg = """ .. nick .. "": " .. msg end
  broadcastToAll(msg, { 0.4, 0.8, 0.4 })

  -- Soft, non-blocking sanity notes -- the app's deck model does not enforce
  -- these either, so we only warn.
  if total ~= 40 and total ~= 0 then
    broadcastToAll("  note: " .. total .. " cards (a legal faction deck is 40).", { 0.8, 0.8, 0.4 })
  end
  reportProblems(list)
end

function reportProblems(list)
  if #list.problems == 0 then return end
  broadcastToAll("  " .. #list.problems .. " line(s) not recognized:", { 1, 0.6, 0.2 })
  for _, p in ipairs(list.problems) do
    broadcastToAll("    " .. p.line, { 1, 0.6, 0.2 })
  end
end

-- ===== relay polling (host only; guests' clients never run this) =====

function relayBase()
  local u = trim(relayURL or "")
  if u == "" then return nil end
  return string.gsub(u, "/+$", "") -- strip trailing slashes
end

function startPolling()
  stopPolling()
  pollOnce()
  pollHandle = Wait.time(pollOnce, POLL_SECONDS, -1)
end

function stopPolling()
  if pollHandle ~= nil then
    Wait.stop(pollHandle)
    pollHandle = nil
  end
end

-- pollOnce fetches the manifest for our table code, then fetches and imports
-- each player's deck whose timestamp advanced since we last spawned it. A
-- cache-busting query defeats any CDN between us and the relay.
function pollOnce()
  local base = relayBase()
  if base == nil then
    broadcastToAll("Deck Importer: no relay URL set.", { 1, 0.6, 0.2 })
    return
  end
  local url = base .. "/t/" .. tableCode .. "?t=" .. tostring(os.time())
  WebRequest.get(url, function(req)
    if req.is_error or req.response_code ~= 200 then
      return
    end
    local ok, manifest = pcall(function() return JSON.decode(req.text) end)
    if not ok or type(manifest) ~= "table" then return end
    for _, player in ipairs(playersToFetch(manifest, watermarks)) do
      fetchPlayerDeck(base, player, manifest[player])
    end
  end)
end

function fetchPlayerDeck(base, player, ts)
  local url = base .. "/t/" .. tableCode .. "/" .. player .. "?t=" .. tostring(os.time())
  WebRequest.get(url, function(req)
    if req.is_error or req.response_code ~= 200 then return end
    -- Record the watermark first: even if a spawn hiccups we never loop on
    -- the same delivery.
    watermarks[player] = ts
    broadcastToAll("Deck from " .. player .. " received.", { 0.5, 0.7, 1 })
    importText(req.text)
  end)
end
